title: RAII: Resource Acquisition Is Initialization
overview: Tying the lifetime of a resource (like memory) to the lifetime of an object. When the object goes out of scope, the resource is freed.
historical_context: Pioneered by Bjarne Stroustrup for C++ as a way to automate resource management without a garbage collector.
mental_model: The resource is the object; the object’s death is the resource’s freedom.
tradeoffs: Requires strict adherence to ownership rules and constructor/destructor idioms.
legacy: The foundation of modern C++ and a major influence on Rust’s ownership model.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Contrast RAII with Garbage Collection in high-performance contexts.
How does C++ Smart Pointers (std::unique_ptr) automate RAII?
Explain the relationship between RAII and the Borrow Checker in Rust.
